seo_h1: Heliodorus of Athens on the Athenian Acropolis

seo.browser_title: Heliodorus of Athens on Eulogikon Greek Text Archive
seo.meta_title: Heliodorus of Athens Ancient Greek Geographical Works
seo.meta_description: Heliodorus of Athens was a 2nd century BCE Hellenistic geographer. His surviving geographical texts. Free to read and download from the Greek library archive.

biography_html:
Heliodorus of Athens was a Greek author of the 2nd century BCE. Beyond his Athenian origin and this chronological placement, deduced from his work, no biographical details survive.

His sole known work is On the Acropolis of Athens, a topographical description of the Acropolis, its monuments, and dedications. The text is now lost, preserved only in fragments cited by later authors. Heliodorus represents a minor contributor to Hellenistic local history and topography.

His significance derives from his work’s later use as a reference by lexicographers and scholiasts for details of Acropolis monuments. These fragments provide valuable evidence for modern reconstructions of the site’s Hellenistic appearance.
